title: Parents of teens who break curfew in D.C. will be prosecuted, DOJ says
description: The Justice Department’s crackdown on crime comes ahead of 250th anniversary events in the nation’s capital.

title: Federal civil rights watchdog wants to stop tracking data on race and sex
description: The EEOC has proposed ending a civil-rights-era program collecting demographic information from private companies.

title: Supreme Court is death knell for Virginia's Democratic-friendly congressional maps
description: The new map was drawn by Democrats and approved by Virginia voters, but the state Supreme Court declared the referendum null and void because lawmakers failed to follow the proper procedures to get the issue on the ballot.

title: Under Trump, State Department language on migration echoes that of white nationalists
description: The State Department is changing the way the U.S. talks about migration and refugees, embracing the "Great Replacement" theory promoted by white nationalists.

title: Immigration hearing casts two different views of Virginia’s Fairfax County
description: Republicans accused Fairfax County Commonwealth’s Attorney Steve Descano of allowing people in the country illegally to get away with serious crimes.

title: Colorado's Democratic governor will let Trump ally Tina Peters out of prison early
description: Gov. Jared Polis' controversial commutation follows a pressure campaign by the Trump administration to free Tina Peters, an ex-county official who was convicted of tampering with election equipment.

title: Tracking who Trump is appointing to fill key administration roles
description: Follow President Trump’s progress filling over 800 positions, among about 1,300 that require Senate confirmation, in this tracker from The Washington Post and the Partnership for Public Service.

title: The Trump administration is planning a prayer event on the National Mall. All but one of the speakers is Christian
description: The event comes as a new survey finds that many Americans aren't comfortable mixing religion and politics.

title: A golden statue of Trump draws mixed reactions at his golf course
description: Online, the glittering statue of Trump has become a flash point. In real life, golfers seem less excited.

title: Can the NFL's Rooney Rule survive the DEI backlash?
description: Florida's attorney general says the NFL's Rooney Rule, which requires teams to interview minority candidates for top jobs, is discriminatory. Trump's EEOC has challenged such policies elsewhere.

title: In pageantry and politics, China summit yields Xi’s goal — equal footing with U.S.
description: The image of peer superpowers during President Trump’s visit displayed a dynamic that analysts say the Chinese have long sought and Americans had resisted.

title: Key takeaways from Trump's China trip
description: President Trump returns to the U.S. after wrapping up his whirlwind trip to China.

title: Death toll in attack on Kyiv apartment building now stands at 24
description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy led an official day of mourning in Kyiv a day after a Russian cruise missile flattened an apartment building. It was one of the deadliest attacks on the capital in the 4-year-old war.

title: After redistricting, what does representation mean to Tennessee voters?
description: What does representation look like for Tennessee voters who were split into three new congressional districts last week? NPR traveled from Memphis into the Nashville suburbs to ask.

title: Reps. Brian Fitzpatrick and Tom Suozzi on rising issue of gerrymandering
description: NPR's Michel Martin talks to Rep. Brian Fitzpatrick, R-Penn., and Rep. Tom Suozzi, D-N.Y., about what they believe is the nation's current greatest problem: gerrymandering.

title: CIA Director John Ratcliffe met with Raul Castro's grandson in Havana, US and Cuban officials say
description: CIA Director John Ratcliffe met with Cuban officials including Raúl Castro's grandson during a high-level visit to the island Thursday, Cuban and U.S. officials said.

title: Supreme Court preserves access to mail distribution of widely used abortion pill
description: The decision maintains access nationwide to medication abortion, the most common way of ending pregnancies in the United States, while litigation continues.

title: Louisiana, at center of voting rights fight, moves to redraw Black House district
description: Thursday’s vote was one of many in Southern states following the Supreme Court’s recent decision to weaken the Voting Rights Act.

title: JD Vance praises Sen. Susan Collins, a GOP moderate and occasional Trump foe
description: The warm words came at a Maine rally as the vice president touted Republican House candidate Paul LePage and highlighted anti-fraud efforts.

title: Xi warns Trump that mishandling Taiwan could spark ‘conflicts’
description: China’s leader made clear his top priority is the fate of the contested island and its U.S. military support, a striking move given President Donald Trump’s effort to mend ties and deliver trade deals.

title: Mapping where the redistricting fight stands and where it’s headed
description: As Trump pushes for a more Republican-friendly House map, more than half a dozen states are potential targets for mid-decade tweaks to congressional boundaries.

title: Rubio is banned from China, but Beijing may have found a way around it
description: Chinese officials are using a different transliterated character for the secretary of state's name, perhaps to allow him to visit without lifting the 2020 ban.
